primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) consistent with sepsis per Sepsis-3 criteria. AKI has progressed from Stage 1 (creatinine 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) within 48 hours. Concurrent findings include anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), hyperbilirubinemia (1.4 mg/dL), and persistent platelet <100 K/uL meeting SOFA coagulation criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 15 hours (excluded outlier) meeting KDIGO Stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score ≥3 (score 4 threshold: <50 K/uL)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no documentation of vasopressor-dependent hypotension or fluid resuscitation status

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and targeted cultures based on suspected source
Begin fluid resuscitation and assess need for vasopressors
Monitor platelet transfusion threshold (<20 K/uL) and coagulation parameters
Consult nephrology for urgent dialysis consideration (AKI Stage 3)
doctor_handoff: Review source control for sepsis, assess response to initial resuscitation, and evaluate for reversible causes of AKI. Verify excluded creatinine outlier was measurement error or represents true acute injury.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
